chapter: Chapter 5: Weavers, Iron Smelters and Factory Owners

questions.question: Who were weavers? Explain their work.
questions.answer: Weavers were skilled workers who made cloth using handlooms. They produced cotton and silk fabrics for local use and trade.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the Indian textile industry.
questions.answer: The Indian textile industry was famous for producing high-quality cotton and silk fabrics that were traded in many parts of the world.

questions.question: Explain how Indian textiles were popular in world markets.
questions.answer: Indian textiles were known for their fine quality, beautiful designs and durability, which made them popular in international markets.

questions.question: Describe the process of weaving cloth using handlooms.
questions.answer: Weaving involves interlacing threads using a handloom to produce cloth.

questions.question: Explain the role of weaving communities in India.
questions.answer: Weaving communities produced cloth and contributed greatly to India's economy and trade.

questions.question: Explain the problems faced by Indian weavers during British rule.
questions.answer: Indian weavers faced problems such as competition from machine-made cloth and loss of markets.

questions.question: Explain why the traditional weaving industry declined.
questions.answer: The traditional weaving industry declined due to the import of cheap machine-made cloth from Britain.

questions.question: Who were iron smelters? Explain their work.
questions.answer: Iron smelters were workers who produced iron from iron ore using traditional furnaces and charcoal.

questions.question: Explain the importance of iron smelting in traditional industries.
questions.answer: Iron smelting produced tools and equipment that were essential for agriculture and daily life.

questions.question: Explain why the iron smelting industry declined during British rule.
questions.answer: The iron smelting industry declined due to forest laws and the availability of cheaper imported iron.

questions.question: Explain the role of forests in traditional iron smelting.
questions.answer: Forests provided wood which was used to make charcoal required for iron smelting.

questions.question: Explain how factory industries developed in India.
questions.answer: Factories using machines were established in cities to produce goods on a large scale.

questions.question: Who were factory owners?
questions.answer: Factory owners were people who invested money to set up factories and produce goods using machines.

questions.question: Explain the difference between handloom and factory production.
questions.answer: Handloom production uses manual labour while factory production uses machines for large-scale manufacturing.

questions.question: Explain the impact of industrialization on traditional industries.
questions.answer: Industrialization introduced machines that replaced many traditional crafts and occupations.

questions.question: Explain how machines changed textile production.
questions.answer: Machines increased production and made cloth cheaper, which affected handloom weavers.

questions.question: Explain why Indian textiles lost their markets.
questions.answer: Indian textiles lost markets due to competition from cheaper British machine-made cloth.

questions.question: Explain the importance of textile mills in India.
questions.answer: Textile mills produced cloth using machines and helped develop modern industries in India.

questions.question: Explain how factory industries affected traditional workers.
questions.answer: Factory industries replaced traditional crafts and forced many workers to change occupations.

questions.question: Explain the role of machines in industrial production.
questions.answer: Machines increased efficiency and allowed factories to produce goods quickly and in large quantities.

questions.question: Explain how traditional industries supported local economies.
questions.answer: Traditional industries provided employment and produced goods used by local communities.

questions.question: Explain how British industrialization affected Indian industries.
questions.answer: British industrialization introduced machine-made goods that replaced many traditional Indian products.

questions.question: Explain the importance of technological change in industries.
questions.answer: Technological change improved production methods and increased efficiency in industries.

questions.question: Explain how factory industries changed economic activities.
questions.answer: Factory industries created new jobs and changed the way goods were produced and traded.

questions.question: Explain the overall impact of industrialization on Indian industries.
questions.answer: Industrialization transformed traditional industries and introduced modern factory-based production systems.
